subject: Leadership team assessment — timeline for the debrief
snippet: Jordan, the Hogan results are back for all eight leaders...
body:
Jordan,

The Hogan results are back for all eight leaders. The team is eager to see where they land before the offsite. Can we hold the debrief in the last week of January and keep the individual readouts to 45 minutes each?

Happy to open our Teams tenant for the sessions.

Julia

subject: Re: Leadership team assessment — timeline for the debrief
snippet: The last week of January works. I'll structure it as...
body:
Julia,

The last week of January works. I'll structure it as a 90-minute team readout first, then individual 45s over two days. The team session is where the real movement happens, so let's protect it.

Send me the Teams links and I'll confirm the slots with Priya.

Jordan

subject: Psychological-safety series — session 3 reschedule
snippet: We need to move the third session. Would October work...
body:
Jordan,

We need to move the third session in the safety series. Two of the desk heads have a regulatory review that swallowed the original date. Would October 9 work for the continuation? I can route the invite through our Outlook tenant.

The first two sessions landed well, for what it's worth. People are still quoting the candor exercise.

Karen

subject: Re: Psychological-safety series — session 3 reschedule
snippet: October 9 is fine. Send the Outlook invite and I'll...
body:
Karen,

October 9 is fine. Send the Outlook invite and I'll hold the morning. I'll rework the opener so we recover the momentum from sessions one and two rather than restarting cold.

Glad the candor exercise stuck. That's the one that usually does.

Jordan

subject: Culture transformation — working session prep
snippet: Ahead of our October working session, I've dropped the...
body:
Jordan,

Ahead of our October 16 working session, I've dropped the engagement survey cuts onto the Monday.com board. The nursing units are where the trust scores dip. I'd like the session to leave the frontline managers with something concrete to run the next morning.

Daniel

subject: Coaching for the three VPs — mid-engagement check
snippet: Two of the three are visibly shifting how they run...
body:
Jordan,

Two of the three are visibly shifting how they run their staff meetings. The third is more guarded, which you predicted. Can we add a short calibration call so I can share what I'm seeing from the outside without stepping on the confidentiality of the sessions?

Elaine

subject: Re: Coaching for the three VPs — mid-engagement check
snippet: Let's do a 30-minute calibration. I'll keep it at the...
body:
Elaine,

Let's do a 30-minute calibration. I'll keep it at the level of patterns, not content, so we stay clean on confidentiality. The guarded one usually turns the corner once the risk of being honest feels lower than the risk of staying quiet. We're close.

Jordan

subject: The Safety Equation — proposal feedback
snippet: I read the revised proposal on the flight back. The...
body:
Jordan,

I read the revised proposal on the flight back. The core argument is strong: psychological safety as a measurable business driver, not a mood. Two editors will respond to that framing. Tighten the comp titles and give me one more sample chapter and I think we can go out in the spring.

Target advance is realistic at the range we discussed.

Rick

subject: Re: The Safety Equation — proposal feedback
snippet: Good. I'll send a cleaner comp list and the measurement...
body:
Rick,

Good. I'll send a cleaner comp list and the measurement chapter, which is the strongest of the set. Manuscript target is still March next year and I'm on pace. Let's line up the follow-up for late October so I'm not scrambling around the fall client load.

Jordan

subject: January invoices out — two payments landed
snippet: Sent the Hawthorne and Vantage invoices. Meridian's...
body:
Jordan,

Sent the Hawthorne and Vantage invoices this morning. Meridian's deposit landed and Crestline's first milestone cleared. I've filed the receipts in the finance folder and flagged the one keynote deposit that's still outstanding.

Priya

subject: Keynote inquiry — regional HR leaders forum
snippet: We'd love to have you open our fall forum with a talk...
body:
Dr. Brock,

We'd love to have you open our fall forum with a talk on psychological safety as a performance lever. Audience is roughly 400 HR and people leaders. Our honorarium range fits your published rate. Are you available in the fourth quarter?

Best,
The Leadership Summit Bureau

subject: Re: Keynote inquiry — regional HR leaders forum
snippet: Thank you. I'm open in the fourth quarter. Copying Priya...
body: Thank you. I'm open in the fourth quarter and the topic is squarely in my lane. Copying Priya, who handles my calendar and contracting. She'll send available dates and the standard rider.\n\nJordan

subject: Leading Out Loud — episode 3 edit ready
snippet: The cut of episode 3 is ready for your review...
body:
Hi Jordan,

The cut of episode 3 is ready for your review. We tightened the open by about ninety seconds and cleaned the room tone from the second guest segment. Show notes draft is attached. Let us know if you want the cold open moved.

The PodBean Pro team

subject: Re: Leading Out Loud — episode 3 edit ready
snippet: Move the cold open to right after the intro sting...
body: Move the cold open to right after the intro sting and keep the ninety-second trim. The show notes read well. Once it's locked I'll schedule the release for a Tuesday. Thanks for the fast turnaround.\n\nJordan

subject: Data set for the safety-and-performance paper
snippet: I've cleaned the second wave of survey data. If you're...
body:
Jordan,

I've cleaned the second wave of survey data. If you're still framing the book chapter around measurable safety, this cut should back you up nicely. Want to co-author the shorter journal version once your manuscript is out of the way?

Brian

subject: Our March session — a prompt to sit with
snippet: Before we meet, one question: where are you saying yes...
body:
Jordan,

Before we meet, one question to sit with: where are you saying yes out of habit rather than intent? You've built a full fall already and it's only March. Bring me your real answer, not the polished one.

Patricia

subject: S-corp — Q4 estimate and mileage log
snippet: Numbers look clean for the quarter. I need your final...
body:
Jordan,

Numbers look clean for the quarter. I need your final mileage log and the home-office square footage confirmed before I close the estimate. If the keynote income lands where you're projecting, we'll want to revisit the owner distribution in the fall.

David

subject: Saturday dinner — who's coming
snippet: I'm doing the braised short ribs. Confirm the count so...
body: I'm doing the braised short ribs Saturday. Confirm the count so I know how much to buy. Megan says she's vegan again this month, so I'll do the mushroom thing on the side. Can you grab a good stout on the way home?\n\nS

subject: Econ paper — mind reading a draft?
snippet: It's on incentive design and I keep circling the same...
body:
Dad,

It's on incentive design and I keep circling the same point without landing it. Would you read a draft this weekend? Also crew has a regatta in the fall I want you and Mom to come to. I'll send dates.

Ryan

subject: Annual physical — scheduling for the fall
snippet: Dr. Hassan's office is opening fall physical slots...
body:
Dear Mr. Brock,

Dr. Hassan's office is opening fall annual-physical slots. Your last visit in January was unremarkable and you're due for the yearly. Please reply with a preferred date and we'll confirm.

Westgate Physicians

subject: Pollinator garden — quote and spring timing
snippet: Here's the estimate for the pollinator beds off the...
body:
Hi Jordan,

Here's the estimate for the pollinator beds off the deck. The design keeps your raised herb boxes and adds native flowering plants along the back line. We'd install once the frost risk passes. Let me know and I'll hold a crew.

Maria, Green Thumb STL

subject: Saturday walk
snippet: Forest Park at eight? Supposed to be cold but clear...
body: Forest Park at eight? Supposed to be cold but clear. I'll bring the coffee if you bring the pace. Grace wants to know if you and Sarah are free for dinner later in the month.\n\nTom

subject: Guest invitation — measuring team safety
snippet: We'd like to have you on to talk about turning safety...
body:
Hi Jordan,

We'd like to have you on the show to talk about turning psychological safety into a metric leaders can actually track. Your recent posts on the topic sparked the idea. We record remotely and can work around your schedule this quarter or next.

The Leadership Lab Cast team

subject: You have been selected for an exclusive coaching grant
snippet: Claim your pre-approved leadership grant today...
body:
Dear Consultant,

You have been selected for an exclusive pre-approved leadership grant. Click now to claim your funds before the window closes.

Regards,
The Grants Desk

body:
Hi Jordan,

The cut of episode 3 is ready for your review. We tightened the open by about ninety seconds and cleaned the room tone from the second guest segment. Show notes draft is attached. Let us know if you want the cold open moved.

The PodBean Pro team

subject: Re: Meridian psychological-safety session reschedule
snippet: Move the cold open to right after the intro sting...
body: Move the cold open to right after the intro sting and keep the ninety-second trim. The show notes read well. Once it's locked I'll schedule the release for a Tuesday. Thanks for the fast turnaround.\n\nJordan

subject: Coaching agreement — Vantage Biotech VPs
snippet: I've cleaned the second wave of survey data. If you're...
body:
Jordan,

I've cleaned the second wave of survey data. If you're still framing the book chapter around measurable safety, this cut should back you up nicely. Want to co-author the shorter journal version once your manuscript is out of the way?

Brian

subject: Podcast guest confirmation — Leading Out Loud
snippet: Before we meet, one question: where are you saying yes...
body:
Jordan,

Before we meet, one question to sit with: where are you saying yes out of habit rather than intent? You've built a full fall already and it's only March. Bring me your real answer, not the polished one.

Patricia

subject: The Safety Equation — revised proposal notes
snippet: Numbers look clean for the quarter. I need your final...
body:
Jordan,

Numbers look clean for the quarter. I need your final mileage log and the home-office square footage confirmed before I close the estimate. If the keynote income lands where you're projecting, we'll want to revisit the owner distribution in the fall.

David

subject: CCE hours summary for December IPC renewal
snippet: I'm doing the braised short ribs. Confirm the count so...
body: I'm doing the braised short ribs Saturday. Confirm the count so I know how much to buy. Megan says she's vegan again this month, so I'll do the mushroom thing on the side. Can you grab a good stout on the way home?\n\nS

subject: Crestline culture transformation — kickoff agenda
snippet: It's on incentive design and I keep circling the same...
body:
Dad,

It's on incentive design and I keep circling the same point without landing it. Would you read a draft this weekend? Also crew has a regatta in the fall I want you and Mom to come to. I'll send dates.

Ryan

subject: Invoice 2026-014 for the Hawthorne engagement
snippet: Dr. Hassan's office is opening fall physical slots...
body:
Dear Mr. Brock,

Dr. Hassan's office is opening fall annual-physical slots. Your last visit in January was unremarkable and you're due for the yearly. Please reply with a preferred date and we'll confirm.

Westgate Physicians

subject: Workshop logistics — on-site AV and room setup
snippet: Here's the estimate for the pollinator beds off the...
body:
Hi Jordan,

Here's the estimate for the pollinator beds off the deck. The design keeps your raised herb boxes and adds native flowering plants along the back line. We'd install once the frost risk passes. Let me know and I'll hold a crew.

Maria, Green Thumb STL

subject: Discovery call recap and proposed scope
snippet: Forest Park at eight? Supposed to be cold but clear...
body: Forest Park at eight? Supposed to be cold but clear. I'll bring the coffee if you bring the pace. Grace wants to know if you and Sarah are free for dinner later in the month.\n\nTom

subject: Keynote inquiry — ALD conference 2027
snippet: We'd like to have you on to talk about turning safety...
body:
Hi Jordan,

We'd like to have you on the show to talk about turning psychological safety into a metric leaders can actually track. Your recent posts on the topic sparked the idea. We record remotely and can work around your schedule this quarter or next.

The Leadership Lab Cast team

subject: Book table order for the St. Louis event
snippet: Claim your pre-approved leadership grant today...
body:
Dear Consultant,

You have been selected for an exclusive pre-approved leadership grant. Click now to claim your funds before the window closes.

Regards,
The Grants Desk

subject: Speaker demo reel and one-sheet attached
snippet: Jordan, the Hogan results are back for all eight leaders...
body:
Jordan,

The Hogan results are back for all eight leaders. The team is eager to see where they land before the offsite. Can we hold the debrief in the last week of January and keep the individual readouts to 45 minutes each?

Happy to open our Teams tenant for the sessions.

Julia

subject: Pre-workshop assessment link for your team
snippet: The last week of January works. I'll structure it as...
body:
Julia,

The last week of January works. I'll structure it as a 90-minute team readout first, then individual 45s over two days. The team session is where the real movement happens, so let's protect it.

Send me the Teams links and I'll confirm the slots with Priya.

Jordan

subject: Quarterly Dinner and Discourse — save the date
snippet: We need to move the third session. Would October work...
body:
Jordan,

We need to move the third session in the safety series. Two of the desk heads have a regulatory review that swallowed the original date. Would October 9 work for the continuation? I can route the invite through our Outlook tenant.

The first two sessions landed well, for what it's worth. People are still quoting the candor exercise.

Karen

subject: Thank you note and next steps after the session
snippet: October 9 is fine. Send the Outlook invite and I'll...
body:
Karen,

October 9 is fine. Send the Outlook invite and I'll hold the morning. I'll rework the opener so we recover the momentum from sessions one and two rather than restarting cold.

Glad the candor exercise stuck. That's the one that usually does.

Jordan

subject: Reference request for the Crestline board
snippet: Ahead of our October working session, I've dropped the...
body:
Jordan,

Ahead of our October 16 working session, I've dropped the engagement survey cuts onto the Monday.com board. The nursing units are where the trust scores dip. I'd like the session to leave the frontline managers with something concrete to run the next morning.

Daniel

subject: Newsletter draft for review — psychological safety
snippet: Two of the three are visibly shifting how they run...
body:
Jordan,

Two of the three are visibly shifting how they run their staff meetings. The third is more guarded, which you predicted. Can we add a short calibration call so I can share what I'm seeing from the outside without stepping on the confidentiality of the sessions?

Elaine

subject: Travel confirmation for the Chicago keynote
snippet: Let's do a 30-minute calibration. I'll keep it at the...
body:
Elaine,

Let's do a 30-minute calibration. I'll keep it at the level of patterns, not content, so we stay clean on confidentiality. The guarded one usually turns the corner once the risk of being honest feels lower than the risk of staying quiet. We're close.

Jordan

subject: Editing turnaround from PodBean on episode four
snippet: I read the revised proposal on the flight back. The...
body:
Jordan,

I read the revised proposal on the flight back. The core argument is strong: psychological safety as a measurable business driver, not a mood. Two editors will respond to that framing. Tighten the comp titles and give me one more sample chapter and I think we can go out in the spring.

Target advance is realistic at the range we discussed.

Rick

subject: Follow-up: leadership team assessment timeline
snippet: Good. I'll send a cleaner comp list and the measurement...
body:
Rick,

Good. I'll send a cleaner comp list and the measurement chapter, which is the strongest of the set. Manuscript target is still March next year and I'm on pace. Let's line up the follow-up for late October so I'm not scrambling around the fall client load.

Jordan

subject: Re: Meridian psychological-safety session reschedule
snippet: Sent the Hawthorne and Vantage invoices. Meridian's...
body:
Jordan,

Sent the Hawthorne and Vantage invoices this morning. Meridian's deposit landed and Crestline's first milestone cleared. I've filed the receipts in the finance folder and flagged the one keynote deposit that's still outstanding.

Priya

subject: Coaching agreement — Vantage Biotech VPs
snippet: We'd love to have you open our fall forum with a talk...
body:
Dr. Brock,

We'd love to have you open our fall forum with a talk on psychological safety as a performance lever. Audience is roughly 400 HR and people leaders. Our honorarium range fits your published rate. Are you available in the fourth quarter?

Best,
The Leadership Summit Bureau

subject: Podcast guest confirmation — Leading Out Loud
snippet: Thank you. I'm open in the fourth quarter. Copying Priya...
body: Thank you. I'm open in the fourth quarter and the topic is squarely in my lane. Copying Priya, who handles my calendar and contracting. She'll send available dates and the standard rider.\n\nJordan
